# LLM Integration Checklist: Platform Mechanics Spike

**Purpose**: Validate TACD-001 requirements for capability-first LLM/tool discovery, evidence reporting, directive-home proof, and eval-plan behavior coverage across Claude Code and Codex.
**Created**: 2026-06-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are both Claude Code and Codex explicitly required to be covered for installed tools, MCP/app connectors, skills/plugins, and repo-local helpers? [Completeness, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-006a]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec require capability-first discovery without hardcoding a vendor-specific MCP list or named optional-tool preference? [Completeness, Spec US2, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-009a]
- [x] CHK003 Are active runtime guidance, prerequisite/user-facing messaging, deterministic/eval expectations, dependency metadata, generated duplicates, historical/provenance, and fixture/test-only references separated for downstream ownership? [Completeness, Spec FR-004, Spec FR-010]
- [x] CHK004 Does the report plan require each runtime-by-capability matrix cell to include an evidence state and reviewer-facing conclusion rather than narrative-only claims? [Completeness, Spec FR-006a, Plan Platform Mechanics Plan]

## Evidence Quality

- [x] CHK005 Are LLM evidence expectations defined for capability path used, citations or local source files, and confidence reporting? [Completeness, Spec FR-009a, Data Model Capability Mechanics Evidence]
- [x] CHK006 Are confidence levels defined with criteria that distinguish high, medium, and low confidence for source-backed, probe-backed, environment-specific, and unresolved mechanics? [Clarity, Spec FR-006c, Plan Confidence Rubric for Mechanics Evidence]
- [x] CHK007 Are probe appendix requirements specific enough to preserve reproducibility while excluding raw inventories, connector lists, local paths, IDs, tokens, and full transcripts? [Coverage, Spec FR-006b, Spec FR-015]
- [x] CHK008 Does the source-first rule prevent the spike from using live probes as a substitute for local repository evidence when source inspection is sufficient? [Consistency, Spec FR-012, Research Local Source Inventory]

## Directive-Home Proof Bar

- [x] CHK009 Is shared reference plus per-agent pointers allowed only when both static pointer coverage and planned functional eval coverage are defined for Claude Code and Codex? [Consistency, Spec FR-007, Plan Directive-Home Recommendation Rule]
- [x] CHK010 Are static proof requirements defined for pointer coverage, pointer target resolution, runtime-specific equivalents, and active named-tool prose outside approved categories? [Completeness, Spec FR-009, Spec SC-003]
- [x] CHK011 Are planned eval scenarios required to prove behavior with observable assertions instead of only proving pointer presence or target resolution? [Measurability, Spec FR-009b, Spec SC-009, Plan Behavior-Observable Eval Plan Requirement]
- [x] CHK012 Does the fallback rule require runtime-specific equivalents when one runtime lacks reliable pointer or eval-plan coverage? [Consistency, Spec FR-007, Data Model Directive-Home Recommendation]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK013 Do TACD-004 eval-plan requirements cover vendor-neutral optional-tool setup, missing-capability fallback, installed-capability selection without vendor preference, evidence reporting, and directive application in a research or codebase-context task? [Coverage, Spec FR-009a]
- [x] CHK014 Are negative or guardrail cases covered where named-tool references are runtime metadata, generated duplicates, historical/provenance, fixture-only, or active prose preferences? [Coverage, Spec FR-004, Spec FR-008]
- [x] CHK015 Are unresolved runtime mechanics required to remain explicitly unresolved rather than becoming implementation assumptions? [Clarity, Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK016 Are downstream handoffs clear enough that TACD-001 reports eval recommendations without editing final enforcement tests or active runtime guidance? [Consistency, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-011]

## Scope Boundaries

- [x] CHK017 Are no-behavior-change boundaries measurable across active runtime guidance, prerequisite behavior, public docs messaging, final enforcement tests, plugin versions, and generated payload semantics? [Measurability, Spec FR-011, Spec SC-005]
- [x] CHK018 Does the report plan keep TACD-001 scoped to the canonical report and appendix probes rather than creating reusable discovery frameworks or final enforcement layers? [Scope, Plan Summary, Constitution VI]
